paper_title: Fossil Specimen Collection and Analysis Methods

methods.description: Collection of fossil specimens, primarily chemosynthetic bivalves, from specific deep-water strata localities on Leyte Island, Philippines.

steps.description: The specimens reported here were collected from four localities on the west coast of the northwestern tip of Leyte Island, Philippines (Fig. 1) (Majima et al. 2007(Majima et al. , 2010)).
steps.searchable_summary: Visit specified geological collection sites in Leyte, Philippines
inputs: List of four localities (Liog-Liog Point, Buhoc Point, Antipolo Point, Cambantug Point)

steps.description: Locate and collect specimens from geological features such as calcareous concretionary blocks, semi-consolidated mudstone, tuffaceous fine-grained sandstone, huge float concretions, variable-sized and complex concretions around burrows, and massive semi-consolidated mudstone beds.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect fossil specimens from various geological features at the sites
inputs: Geological features at sites (concretions, mudstone, sandstone, burrows)

steps.description: Note specific details about the collection, such as the block sampled extensively at Liog-Liog Point.
steps.searchable_summary: Document specific collection context and details
inputs: Specific features of collection (e.g., block sampled)

methods.description: Application of microfossil, nannofossil, and planktonic foraminifer analyses to determine the age of the fossil-bearing strata at the collection localities.

steps.description: Collect samples of the matrix or beds surrounding the fossil occurrences at each locality.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect geological samples for biostratigraphic analysis

steps.description: Perform microfossil analyses on collected samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Submit or perform microfossil analysis on samples

steps.description: Perform nannofossil analyses on collected samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Submit or perform nannofossil analysis on samples

steps.description: Perform planktonic foraminifer analyses on collected samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Submit or perform planktonic foraminifer analysis on samples

steps.description: Interpret the results from these analyses to determine the age of the strata (e.g., late Pliocene, early Pleistocene, specific nannofossil zones like NN15).
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret biostratigraphic analysis results to determine geological age

steps.description: Evaluate potential issues like reworking of micro- and nannofossils based on conflicting results between different dating methods.
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate discrepancies and potential reworking based on dating results
inputs: Conflicting dating results (e.g., nannofossil vs foraminifer ages)

methods.description: Measurement of the dimensions of collected fossil specimens.

steps.description: Measure the dimensions of collected specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure dimensions of collected fossil specimens

steps.description: Round recorded measurements to the nearest first decimal place.
steps.searchable_summary: Round specimen measurements to one decimal place
